name: Cristina's Brownies
recipeIngredient: 1 pound walnut halves or large pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust a rack one-third up from the bottom of the oven and preheat to 350 degrees.
2. Line a 13 X 9 X 2-inch pan with foil, turning the pan upside down and molding the foil to fit, including the sides. Place a piece of butter in the pan (on the foil) and put it in the oven until melted. Remove from heat and spread the melted butter evenly over the foil.
3. Sift together cocoa powder, flour, and salt into a large bowl and set aside.
4. In a 3-quart heavy-bottomed saucepan over low heat, melt the butter. Once melted, remove the pan from heat and stir in sugar. Add eggs two or three at a time, then vanilla extract. Pour this butter mixture into the sifted dry ingredients all at once.
5. Beat on low speed (mixer) or by hand, scraping the bowl as necessary, until fully mixed. Stir in walnuts.
6. Turn the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 50 to 60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the middle comes out clean with a bit of chocolate clinging to it. Avoid overbaking; they should be moist.
7. Allow cooling at room temperature. When the bottom of the pan is warm, cover it with wax paper and then a flat cookie sheet or board. Carefully remove the pan and foil from the oven.
8. Turn the cake right side up in the refrigerator overnight. Cover with a cookie sheet or board and turn it again, leaving it right side up.
9. Using a long, sharp knife, cut the cake into 24 extra-large brownies (or smaller as preferred).
